actionable_feedback: Include a plain-text, machine-parsable export of workbook calculations: either per-sheet CSV exports and/or a formulas dump (sheet-name,cell,formula,value). This allows graders that lack openpyxl to validate formulas and numeric results.
Add a short worked example in engineering_priority.md linking one final normalized score back to the exact raw input cells and the prior_alpha used (document prior_alpha source/value and rationale).

Provide a one-click runnable reproducibility helper (macro, minimal Python script, or shell command) that re-runs the sensitivity +/-5% sweep and remediation scenarios and writes the outputs (CSV or JSON).

Explicitly document the threshold interpretation in both the markdown and workbook (e.g., state 'classification uses cumulative normalized percentage at rank <= 70.0 => P1_Core70'), and add a validation cell that asserts the interpretation used.

Document justification for targeting remediation only at top contributor (R3) or add an alternative scenario showing distributed remediation effects, and annotate in-workbook comments/cells that implement that assumption.

Unify rubric_item_scores representations in grader output (ensure semantic_correctness_score fields appear consistently in the canonical rubric_item_scores block) to avoid ambiguity for downstream consumers.
artifact_specific_comments: engineering_priority.md: clear and well-structured; include explicit prior_alpha numeric value and short worked example mapping one record's final normalized score to input cells.
yield_loss_bayesian_pareto.xlsx: contains formula-filled sheets per trace, but provide CSV/formulas dump to enable cell-level inspection in constrained environments.
evidence_refs: trace_summary:agent_trace (from Trace summary JSON and ActualOutputs)
feedback_summary: The submission meets the artifact contract and demonstrates correct high-level methods (Bayesian estimator, Pareto cumulative classification, sensitivity sweep, remediation matrix) and passes deterministic and semantic checks. Two practical issues reduce verifiability: (1) the grader/verifier could not parse the xlsx in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), preventing cell-level formula inspection and re-run of perturbation sensitivity; (2) minor representational/metadata gaps (ambiguous source of prior_alpha, single explicit assumption about cumulative threshold interpretation, and inconsistent placement of semantic score fields in grader output) that make offline verification slightly more work. These are fixable with small, targeted changes to the deliverables and metadata.

grading_limitations: openpyxl unavailable in grading environment prevented cell-level formula inspection and workbook re-execution; grader relied on engineering_priority.md and trace.
recommended_actions: Provide a parsable export of the workbook (CSV per sheet and a formulas dump).
Embed explicit row-level citations and a worked example mapping one normalized score back to raw inputs and prior_alpha.
Add a reproducibility script or macro to run the sensitivity sweep and remediation scenarios and output CSV/JSON results.
Clarify and document threshold interpretation and remediation-targeting assumptions in both workbook and markdown.
Standardize rubric_item_scores structure in result metadata.
extra_model_fields.reproducibility_rationale: Reproducibility is high at the conceptual and artifact-presence level (markdown + trace + generated workbook) but not fully demonstrated at cell/formula level in environments lacking openpyxl-providing CSV/formulas and a reproducibility script will close this gap.
verifier_warnings: Ensure the xlsx is openpyxl-readable or provide CSV/formulas dump for robust verification.
Minor inconsistency detected in how semantic_correctness_score fields were represented in nested grader output blocks-unify representation.
